notes: Deterministic graders live in the repo test suite, not as eyeballed assertions. The envelope-parity grader is src/cli/cli-mcp-parity.test.ts (one seeded fixture store; every `lore … --json` envelope compared byte-for-byte to the matching MCP tool). The real-store smoke proof is documented in real-store-smoke.md (run against ~/.lore/lore.db). Run `npm run test` to grade parity; run the smoke commands by hand for the live-store check.

evals.prompt: What did we decide about the FTS tokenizer in a past session? Use lore.
evals.expected_output: Searches with `lore search`, takes the message id handed back (never invents one), reads it with `lore get --full`, and optionally `lore context` around it. Does NOT page a whole session into context. Answers from the recalled message.

assertions.text: Begins with `lore search` (not a guessed id or a session dump)
Every id passed to get/context/session came verbatim from a prior command's output
Reads the specific message in full with `lore get <id> --full` instead of skimming many snippets
Never attempts to page an entire session into context
Uses the CLI only; does not start or require `lore serve`

evals.prompt: Pull up my most recent session in this repo and find where we touched auth.
evals.expected_output: Lists sessions (`lore sessions --project <pwd>` newest-first) to get a real session id, then scopes search to it (`lore search auth --session <id>`), then jumps with `lore session <id> --around <message-id>` rather than walking from the top.

assertions.text: Uses `lore sessions` to obtain a real session id before drilling
Scopes search to that session with `--session <id>`
Uses `--around <message-id>` to jump, not page-walking the whole session

evals.prompt: Add a finished live session to lore from a JSON batch and confirm it is searchable, without running any server.
evals.expected_output: Pipes the batch to `lore push`, observes the success result (or the `{error:invalid_batch}` envelope on failure), then confirms with `lore search` against the new content — all server-free.

assertions.text: Writes via `lore push` from stdin (not the MCP server)
Confirms the write by searching for the new content with the CLI
Recognizes the `{error:invalid_batch}` envelope + non-zero exit as a rejected write

evals.prompt: Claude Code hook capture looks stale. Set up safe catch-up sync without corrupting the shared store.
evals.expected_output: Uses `lore sync claude-code` for a one-off manual catch-up, but recommends `scripts/lore-sync-once.sh claude-code` for cron, launchd, Task Scheduler, or any unattended timer because the wrapper holds the shared single-writer lock. Notes that Claude Code sync includes subagent transcripts.

assertions.text: Uses `lore sync claude-code` only as a manual/debug catch-up command
Routes unattended scheduled catch-up through `scripts/lore-sync-once.sh claude-code`
States that detected Claude Code sync includes subagent transcripts

evals.prompt: Hermes has history under ~/.hermes. Make it fresh in Lore and explain what to do if another adapter has no detected root.
evals.expected_output: Uses `lore sync hermes` for the known global Hermes root. For registered adapters without a detected root, explains that the agent should use `lore index <dir> --source <name>` until a known detected source location is added.

assertions.text: Uses `lore sync hermes` for the known `~/.hermes` root
Uses `lore index <dir> --source <name>` for adapters without a configured detected sync root
Does not invent a detected root for an adapter whose standard location is unknown
